assets.title: Track Data Format
required_mentions: Describe the root-database column layout for track payloads.
Keep the explanation that trackdata stores trackTitle, mixBinary, noteBinary, and cachedMixList.
required_claims: The binary fields are produced by the editor translation layer and are not intended to be edited by hand.

assets.title: Music Meta Data Format
required_mentions: Describe the root-database column layout for music metadata.
Keep the explanation that musdata stores title, composer, musicPath, bpmBinary, bpm, and firstBeat.
required_claims: firstBeat is used as the PCM-frame offset where the first musical beat begins for that audio asset.

assets.title: Mix Data Format
required_mentions: Keep the compact field table for MixArgs because it remains useful when authoring or reviewing raw data rows.

assets.title: Mix Data Table
required_mentions: Explain how first, second, and third are interpreted for each mix type.
Preserve the note that the table keeps older author-facing labels because they are still useful.
required_claims: Current source names such as BPM_CONTROL and OSC_FILTER map to the older bpmControl and OCS_Filter spellings shown in the table.

assets.title: Note Data Format
required_mentions: Keep the note-format field table as the compact reference for NoteArgs rows.

assets.title: Interpolation Keywords
required_mentions: Keep the explanation that MixArgs.first often carries the interpolation selector.
Keep the explanation that MixArgs.second often carries the comma-separated control values.
required_claims: Older docs referred to these control values as 8PointValues, and the term remains useful for understanding the current runtime parser.

assets.title: Mix JSON Keys
required_mentions: Keep the table that explains the meaning of each mix JSON key.
Do not reduce this section to a plain key list without meanings.

assets.title: EightPointValues Example
required_mentions: Explain that the image should be read as eight control points stretched across the event span from the start beat tuple to the end beat tuple.
required_claims: This is the older model used to explain automated filter, EQ, volume, and other FX changes, and it still matches the current runtime parser.
With ITPL_FLAT, older single-value examples still match the flat interpolator concept in the current tree.
